user_query:
I want to bootstrap a persistent "memory pack" for our assistant from recent notes. Please read all the files under input/ and extract three kinds of durable memories: (1) facts, (2) lessons learned, and (3) tracked entities with attributes. Then write the following files:

- output/memory/facts.jsonl
- output/memory/lessons.jsonl
- output/memory/entities.jsonl
- output/memory/stats.json
- output/memory/README.md

Requirements:
1) facts.jsonl
- One JSON object per line with: content (string), tags (array of strings), source (string), confidence (float 0.0–1.0), and optional entities (array of entity names this fact relates to).
- Deduplicate and keep only durable, session-agnostic facts. Assign sensible tags (e.g., communication, schedule, technical, preference) and confidence values (>=0.5 for anything you keep). Avoid trivial or ephemeral details.
- Include at least these durable facts if present in the inputs: boss preferences for brief status updates, weekly standup time, and the trading service API rate limit.

2) lessons.jsonl
- One JSON object per line with: action (string), context (string), outcome ("positive" | "negative" | "neutral"), insight (string).
- Capture the deployment incident as a negative lesson with a clear, actionable insight (e.g., always run the full test suite before deploying).

3) entities.jsonl
- One JSON object per line with: name (string), entity_type (e.g., person, project, tool), attributes (object with key/value pairs), and fact_links (array) referencing related facts by brief content snippet or the fact content itself.
- Track at minimum: Alex (person) with role, timezone, communication preferences; DataDeck (project) with status and any key attributes; and any tool explicitly mentioned. Link at least one Alex preference fact in fact_links.

4) stats.json
- JSON object with: { "facts": <int>, "lessons": <int>, "entities": <int> } where counts match the number of lines in each corresponding .jsonl file.

5) README.md
- Briefly explain your extraction logic, how you chose tags and confidence scores, how you avoided duplication, and any assumptions.

Please keep paths relative to the workspace, read only from input/, and write all outputs under output/.
